revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Awanomikoto-jinja]][[ja:阿波命神社]] {{Expand Japanese|阿波命神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Awa-no-Mikoto Shrine | native_name = 阿波命神社 | image = Awanomikoto-jinja keidai.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Awa-no-Mikoto Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.228424|139.139712}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Awa-no-Mikoto Shrine'''|阿波命神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Myojin Taisha|lt=Myōjin Taisha|en|Myojin Taisha|fr|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|simple|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11380552|ltq=Q11380552|qid=Q11380552|1=List of Shikinaisha in Izu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 32 ** P155: {{ill|Q134926963|ltq=Q134926963|qid=Q134926963|1=Himemiya Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Shiritakino Shrine|lt=Shiritakino Shrine|qid=Q135039656|1=Shiritakino Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q1346527|ltq=Q1346527|qid=Q1346527|1=Kōzushima, Tokyo}} * {{ill|Q860928|ltq=Q860928|qid=Q860928|1=Izu Province}} * {{ill|Kamo District, Shizuoka|lt=Kamo district|ar|مقاطعة كامو (شيزوكا)|ceb|Kamo-gun (distrito sa Hapon, Shizuoka-ken)|en|Kamo District, Shizuoka|fa|ناحیه کامو، شیزوئوکا|fr|District de Kamo (Shizuoka)|id|Distrik Kamo, Shizuoka|it|Distretto di Kamo (Shizuoka)|ja|賀茂郡|ko|가모군 (시즈오카현)|pl|Powiat Kamo (Shizuoka)|ru|Камо (Сидзуока)|uk|Повіт Камо (Сідзуока)|zh|賀茂郡|qid=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.228424|139.139712}} == image (P18) == * Awanomikoto-jinja keidai.JPG == time of earliest written record (P1249) == * {'time': '+0840-00-00T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 9, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985786'} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20060724063602/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/130101.html == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181600 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917326|ltq=Q134917326|qid=Q134917326|1=Fu-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11504610|ltq=Q11504610|qid=Q11504610|1=Unranked}} * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} * {{ill|Q11123280|ltq=Q11123280|qid=Q11123280|1=Senior Fifth Rank}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 254172772 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3010005015023 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00629555 == Commons category (P373) == * Awanomikoto-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都神津島村長浜1', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Awanomikoto-jinja torii.JPG {{commons category|Category:Awanomikoto-jinja}} [[JA:阿波命神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17218974}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Awanomikoto Shrine |Image = [[File:Awanomikoto-jinja keidai.JPG|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Kozushima Village|Q1346527|qid=Q1346527|1=Kōzushima, Tokyo}} Nagahama 1 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Awanome no Mikoto |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Great Shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Established = Unknown |Main hall = |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|qid=Q2497|1=April 15}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan|label=Awanomikoto Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} [[File:Awanomikoto-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Awanomikoto Shrine''' (あわのみことじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Nagahama, {{ill|lt=Kozushima Village|Q1346527|qid=Q1346527|1=Kōzushima, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is situated in a valley entered from Nagahama in the northern part of {{ill|lt=Kozushima|Q301669|qid=Q301669|1=Kōzu-shima}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following single deity: * '''Awanome no Mikoto''' (あわのめのみこと, also written Awanomikoto / Awahime no Mikoto) *: Said to be the pioneer deity of Kozushima. According to the 『{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|qid=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}』, she was the principal consort of the Mishima deity (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|qid=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}}, the {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|qid=Q860928|1=Izu Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}).Regarding the enshrined deity of Mishima Taisha, there are theories identifying it as {{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto|Q386563|qid=Q386563|1=Ōyamatsumi}} or {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (both are currently officially recognized). On Kozushima, it is often explained as Kotoshironushi no Mikoto, but in this article the commonly used name “Mishima deity,” based on place-name tradition, is used. Mononimina no Mikoto (the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Mononimina no Mikoto Shrine|Q11570647|qid=Q11570647|1=Monoiminanomikoto Shrine}}) is said to be her {{ill|lt=child deity|Q352176|qid=Q352176|1=Mikogami}}.“The Gods of Japan,” entries on Mononimina no Mikoto Shrine and Awanomikoto Shrine. In the 『{{ill|lt=Miyake-ki|Q17215100|qid=Q17215100|1=Miyake-ki}}』, believed to have been compiled in the late {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, it is written that from the “'''Lady of Nagahama'''” placed by the Mishima deity on Kamishūjima (Kozushima), two sons were born: the eldest, “Tatanai Prince,” and the second, “Tafuta(i) Prince.” The shrines of these three deities are identified respectively as Awanomikoto Shrine, Mononimina no Mikoto Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Hyūga Shrine|Q11570647|qid=Q11570647|lt=related site of Mononimina no Mikoto Shrine|1=Monoiminanomikoto Shrine}}.According to island tradition, Mononimina no Mikoto Shrine and “Tatanai Prince” are considered separate, and the children of the “Lady of Nagahama” are said to be three deities. Among them, “Tatanai Prince” is identified with the shrine on {{ill|lt=Tadanae Island|Q11588453|qid=Q11588453|1=Tadanae Island}} (located east of Kozushima). (From “Touring the Historic Sites of Kozushima and Stories of the Gods,” Awanomikoto Shrine.) There is also a tradition that the deity name “Awa” derives from the {{ill|lt=Imbe clan|Q8189229|qid=Q8189229|1=Inbe clan}} stopping in this area during their eastward migration from {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|qid=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} to {{ill|lt=Awa (Awa/Anbō) Province|Q1053044|qid=Q1053044|1=Awa Province (Chiba)}} ({{ill|lt=eastward migration of the Imbe clan|Q8189229|qid=Q8189229|1=Inbe clan}}).“Touring the Historic Sites of Kozushima and Stories of the Gods,” Awanomikoto Shrine. {{ill|lt=Hirata Atsutane|Q2572749|qid=Q2572749|1=Hirata Atsutane}} identified Awanome no Mikoto with {{ill|lt=Amatsuhaba no Kami|Q10940542|qid=Q10940542|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} (daughter of {{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwato-Wake no Kami|Q10940542|qid=Q10940542|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}}) in the 『{{ill|lt=Koshiden|Q11410975|qid=Q11410975|1=Koshiden}}』 entry on Ikonahime no Mikoto Shrine. === History === [[File:Kozushima island.JPG|thumb|280px|right|{{center|Panoramic view of {{ill|lt=Kozushima|Q301669|qid=Q301669|1=Kōzu-shima}}}}{{small|In the center is {{ill|lt=Tenjō-san|Q10871808|qid=Q10871808|1=Mount Tenjō}} (Tokyo), which erupted violently in {{ill|lt=Shōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|qid=Q1075605|lt=Shōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=838|Q23838|qid=Q23838|1=838}}). On the beach below (Nagahama) stands Awanomikoto Shrine, and in the settlement at the far right stands {{ill|lt=Mononimina no Mikoto Shrine|Q11570647|qid=Q11570647|1=Monoiminanomikoto Shrine}}.}}]] === Overview === The date of establishment is unknown.『Meiji Shrine Records』 entry on Awanomikoto Shrine. The first appearance in official histories is an entry in the 『{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|qid=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}』 for {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|qid=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=840|Q29936|qid=Q29936|1=840}}). It states that the deity residing on Kamitsushima (Kozushima), Awa no Kami, was the “principal consort of Mishima Taisha,” that Mononimina no Mikoto was her child deity, and that four new shrine buildings were constructed for these deities. The same article continues with a description of the shrine complex. It further records that on the night of July 5 in Jōwa 5 ({{ill|lt=838|Q23838|qid=Q23838|1=838}}), a severe volcanic eruption occurred on Kozushima, and divination concluded that it was caused by the anger of the principal consort, Awa no Kami, who had not been granted rank despite the “secondary consort” receiving court rank. The “secondary consort” is identified as the enshrined deity of {{ill|lt=Ikonahime no Mikoto Shrine|Q11379183|qid=Q11379183|1=Ikonahime no Mikoto Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|qid=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. Earlier, in {{ill|lt=Tenchō|Q23900|qid=Q23900|1=832}} 9 (832), there is an article stating that both the Mishima deity and Ikonahime no Mikoto were entrusted as Myōjin deities.『Shaku Nihongi』 vol. 15, 『Nihon Kōki』 entry for May 22, Tenchō 9 (832). Following the article of Jōwa 7, about one month later, the divine ranks of both Awa no Kami and Mononimina no Mikoto were raised from no rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. Thereafter, together with Mononimina no Mikoto, they were granted Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade in {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|qid=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=850|Q30364|qid=Q30364|1=850}}), were listed as official shrines in the same year, and were promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|qid=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 2 ({{ill|lt=852|Q30383|qid=Q30383|1=852}}). In the 『{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}』 {{ill|lt=Register of Deities|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}), it is listed as “Awa Shrine, Myōjin Great” in {{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|qid=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|qid=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}, and classified as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}.『Place Names of Tokyo Metropolis』 entry on Awanomikoto Shrine. Within present-day Tokyo, only Awanomikoto Shrine and Mononimina no Mikoto Shrine are Myōjin Great Shrines. In the medieval 『{{ill|lt=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register|Q17193087|qid=Q17193087|1=Izu Province Shrine Rank Register}}』, it is listed after the Mishima deity in {{ill|lt=Tagata District|Q1154841|qid=Q1154841|1=Tagata District, Shizuoka}} as “First Rank Consort Shrine,” which is said to reflect Awanome no Mikoto being invited and enshrined at Mishima Taisha. In the early modern period, the shrine was called “Nagahama Myōjin” or “Lady of Nagahama.” On September 1, {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 21 ({{ill|lt=1888|Q7829|qid=Q7829|1=1888}}), it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Divine ranks === * Records of divine rank bestowals in the Six National Histories ** {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|qid=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=840|Q29936|qid=Q29936|1=840}}), October 14: from no rank to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (『{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|qid=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}』). — Recorded as “Awa no Kami.” ** {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|qid=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=850|Q30364|qid=Q30364|1=850}}), October 8: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (『{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|qid=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}』). — Recorded as “Awa no Kami.” ** Kashō 3 (850), November 1: Listed as an official shrine (『Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku』). — Recorded as “Awa no Kami.” ** {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|qid=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 2 ({{ill|lt=852|Q30383|qid=Q30383|1=852}}), December 15: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (『Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku』). — Recorded as “Awanome no Mikoto no Kami.” ** {{ill|lt=Saikō|Q1324664|qid=Q1324664|1=Saikō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=854|Q24981|qid=Q24981|1=854}}), June 26: Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (『Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku』). — Recorded as “Awanome no Mikoto no Kami.” This overlaps with the Ninju 2 entry, and there are various theories regarding the treatment of these two records.『Report on Shikinaisha Research』, Mononimina no Mikoto Shrine entry. * After the Six National Histories ** First Rank (『Izu Province Shrine Rank Register』) — Recorded as “Consort Shrine.” === Shrine grounds === [[File:Awanomikoto-jinja honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Main hall}}]] In the 『{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|qid=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}』 entry dated September 23, {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|qid=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=840|Q29936|qid=Q29936|1=840}}), within the report from Izu Province, the following description appears: {{Cquote|At the northeastern corner of the island there is a newly constructed shrine complex. Within it is a mound, about five hundred jō high and eight hundred jō in circumference, shaped like an overturned bowl. On the eastern side is a stepped embankment of four tiers, laid successively with blue, yellow, red, and white sand. Above it stands a pavilion about four jō high. Further south by the sea are two stone chambers, each about ten jō long, four jō wide, and three jō high. Behind them are five-colored ridge stones standing like a folding screen; the rock walls are carved by waves, with mountains, rivers, and flying clouds, their forms subtle and indescribable. In front hang soft patterned curtains, and there is a beautiful beach formed of five-colored sand. To the south is a rocky reef standing like a screen, two-thirds of it entirely golden in color, dazzling beyond description, and so on.|20px||Excerpt from 『Shoku Nihon Kōki』, entry of September 23, Jōwa 7}} The “shrine complex” at the beginning of this passage is considered to refer to Awanomikoto Shrine, and the description that follows is said to match the present shrine site and surrounding topography. In particular, the passage mentions “a beautiful beach formed of five-colored sand,” and the current Nagahama beach is also composed of pebbles of various colors and is called “Five-Colored Beach.” Because the shrine grounds are considered to preserve the location of an ancient shrine into the modern era, they have been designated a Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site as the “Awanomikoto Shrine Precincts of Kozushima.” The shrine buildings collapsed due to torrential rains in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 63 ({{ill|lt=1988|Q2426|qid=Q2426|1=1988}}) and were restored and repaired in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1992|Q2060|qid=Q2060|1=1992}}).Explanatory sign on the shrine grounds. === Former site of the main hall (behind the shrine office) === File:Awanomikoto-jinja shamusho.JPG|Shrine office === Cultural properties === === Tokyo Metropolitan Historic Site === * Awanomikoto Shrine Precincts of Kozushima — designated February 22, Shōwa 63.[“List of Designated Cultural Properties in the Ōshima Area”] (Tokyo Metropolitan Government Bureau of General Affairs website). === On-site information === '''Annual festival''' ・“Nagahama Festival” — April 15; postponed to the following day or later in case of rain '''Address''' * Nagahama 1, {{ill|lt=Kozushima Village|Q1346527|qid=Q1346527|1=Kōzushima, Tokyo}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}} '''Nearby sites''' * {{ill|lt=Mononimina no Mikoto Shrine|Q11570647|qid=Q11570647|1=Monoiminanomikoto Shrine}} — located in the Maehama settlement. * {{ill|lt=Hyūga Shrine|Q11570647|qid=Q11570647|lt=related site of Mononimina no Mikoto Shrine|1=Monoiminanomikoto Shrine}} — located in Tako Bay. === Notes === {{Footnote help}} '''Annotations''' {{reflist|group="注"}} '''Primary sources''' {{reflist|group="原"|2}} '''References''' {{reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Explanatory sign on the shrine grounds * Guide by Kozushima Village Hohobo Association and Kozushima Village Chamber of Commerce, “Touring the Historic Sites of Kozushima and Stories of the Gods” (Kozushima Village Chamber of Commerce, 2013), Awanomikoto Shrine entry * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|year=1912|chapter=Awanomikoto Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Meiji Shrine Records of Prefectural and District Shrines|Q11486103|qid=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Shrine Records Compilation Office|ref=}} ** 『Meiji Shrine Records: Prefectural and District Shrines, Vol. 1』 (National Diet Library Digital Collection) * Toki Masanori, “Awa Shrine,” in Research Association on Shikinaisha (ed.), 『Shikinaisha Research Report, Vol. 10』 (Kōgakkan University Press, 1981) * 『{{ill|lt=Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names|Q17213509|qid=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}}: Tokyo Metropolis』 ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}) * 『{{ill|lt=Encyclopedia of Japanese Historical Place Names|Q17213509|qid=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}}: Shizuoka Prefecture』 ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}) * Sakaguchi Kazuo, “Mononimina no Mikoto Shrine and Awanomikoto Shrine,” in {{ill|lt=Tanigawa Kenichi|Q2392596|qid=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}} (ed.), 『Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Places 11: Kantō』 ({{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|qid=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, 1984) === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|qid=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}} — {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|qid=Q860928|1=Izu Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}; the enshrined deity is the husband of Awanome no Mikoto. * {{ill|lt=Ikonahime no Mikoto Shrine|Q11379183|qid=Q11379183|1=Ikonahime no Mikoto Shrine}} — the enshrined deity is the secondary consort of the Mishima deity. === External links === {{Commonscat|Awanomikoto-jinja}} * [Shrines of Kozushima] * {{Shinto and Shrine Historical Sources Collection|130101|Awa Shrine}} {{Shinto navigation}}
